approval.note: Do not infer WCAG conformance from automated checks alone. Preserve the public globe, filters and proposal flow while adding exact browser-mode evidence, bounded keyboard-path tests, exact-head review, green CI, Pages deployment and public readback.

metadata.source: Commonworld PR #104 closeout and validator-hardening review follow-up
metadata.observed_limit: The existing browser and validator evidence does not establish forced-colors behaviour, increased-contrast behaviour, complete keyboard-only navigation or visible-focus integrity across the public Commonworld surfaces.

title: Verify forced-colors, increased-contrast and keyboard focus across Commonworld
goal: Make the public globe, discovery filters and Commons proposal path resilient under forced-colors and increased-contrast preferences, and prove that all interactive controls remain keyboard-reachable with visible, unclipped focus and correct semantic state without claiming broader accessibility conformance than the evidence supports.

acceptance.assertion: With forced-colors active, the globe controls, layer and search controls, presence filters, proposal fields, validation messages and primary actions remain visible, distinguishable and operable without relying only on author-defined color.
Where the browser exposes an increased-contrast preference, the public surfaces preserve readable text, boundaries, selected states and focus indicators without clipping, overlap or loss of meaning.

A keyboard-only browser path reaches every public interactive control in logical order, operates disclosures, filters, globe/layer navigation and the proposal form, and encounters no focus trap or unreachable control.
Every keyboard-focusable control has a visible focus indication that is not hidden by overlays, viewport edges, scrolling containers or active-state styling in the tested viewport matrix.
Labels and checked, selected, expanded, disabled, invalid and error states remain programmatically exposed after any CSS or interaction repair.

The closeout records exact browsers, modes and viewport profiles, automated and manual evidence, repaired findings, explicit nonclaims, reviewed diff identity, CI, Pages deployment and public readback.
